global.a: Local authorities in this cluster have a high dependency ratio, high percentage of residents who are not religious, high personal wellbeing and high greenhouse gas emissions. These areas have low digital connectivity and a low percentage of employment in services. This cluster is made up of remote, rural areas and is most prevalent in Wales, Scotland and the South West.
global.b: Local authorities in this cluster have a high percentage of children in relative poverty and high level of gigabit capable broadband availability. These areas have a below average employment rate, a low rate of active businesses and a lower rate of housing completions. This cluster is made up of largely urban areas, including many coastal towns, and is most prevalent across the North, Midlands, South Wales and Northern Ireland.
global.c: Local authorities in this cluster have the fastest growing population, a high employment rate and a high rate of housing completions. These areas are low for guest nights stayed, percentage of children in relative poverty and have a low proportion of private renters. This cluster contains a mix of rural and urban areas and is generally situated in the commuter belt around major urban settlements; this cluster is most prevalent in the East and South East of England.
global.d: Local authorities in this cluster have a high percentage of employment in services, a high proportion of households that are renting privately and generally high results in the core economic indicators in the model including, high-growth businesses, active businesses, gross value added and gross median weekly pay. These areas are more ethnically diverse, have a low proportion of residents who are not religious and a low dependency ratio. Most of these areas in this cluster are London local authorities, with a small number of other urban centres across the UK also present.

economic.a: Local authorities in this cluster have a high percentage of children in relative poverty, low rate of active businesses, low gross median weekly pay, low employment rate, low gross value added per hour worked and a low rate of housing completions. This cluster contains a mix of rural and urban areas and is most prevalent in Wales, the North East and Yorkshire and The Humber.
economic.b: Local authorities in this cluster have a high rate of housing completions and a high percentage of employment in construction. These areas have a low percentage of employment in services, a low proportion of private renters and a low proportion of high growth businesses. This cluster contains a mixture of rural and urban areas and is most prevalent in Scotland, the Midlands and the South West.
economic.c: Local authorities in this cluster have a high employment rate, high gross value added and above average gross median weekly pay. These areas have a low percentage of children in relative poverty and lower than average proportion of private renters. This cluster contains a mix of rural and urban areas and is generally situated in the commuter belt around major urban settlements; this cluster is most prevalent in the East and South East of England.
economic.d: Local authorities in this cluster have a high rate of active businesses, a high percentage of employment in services, more high growth businesses and high gross median weekly pay. These areas have a low proportion of private renters and a low percentage of employment in construction. Most of these areas in this cluster are London local authorities, with a small number of other urban centres across the UK also present.

demographic.a: Local authorities in this cluster are more ethnically diverse, have a low dependency ratio, a low percentage of residents who are not religious and an above average total population. Most of these areas in this cluster are London local authorities, with a small number of other urban centres across the UK also present.
demographic.b: Local authorities in this cluster have a high total population, above average population growth and are more ethnically diverse than average. This cluster contains a mix of rural and urban areas and is most prevalent in the North West and Yorkshire and The Humber.
demographic.c: Local authorities in this cluster have high population growth, an above average dependency ratio and a low total population. This cluster contains a mix of rural and urban areas and is most prevalent in the South East and East of England.
demographic.d: Local authorities in this cluster have a high percentage of white residents, a high proportion of residents who are not religious, low population growth and a low total population. This cluster is largely made up of rural areas and is most prevalent in Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland.
